{{Quote|Destroy him, Wicket! The Ice Demon commands you!|Stagorr, ordering Wicket W. Warrick to kill Logray|<ref name="E6" />}}
'''Ice Demons''' were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with [[Water/Legends|ice]]-like traits, possessing bodies that appeared similar to white [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]], as well as resistance to frigid temperatures. They had long, curved horns and sharp, icicle-like [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. Their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were black with white centers, and their feet ended in sharp spikes.

At least one member of the [[Species/Legends|species]], the [[Magic/Legends|sorcerer]] known as [[Stagorr]], lived on the forest moon of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Several years before [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Stagorr fought in a [[Skirmish on Endor|duel]] with the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]] and was banished to the inside of [[Ice Mountain]]. The Ice Demon hatched a scheme to lure Logray back to free him, but it backfired when [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]] used fire magic to melt some ice, douse the Ice Demon in water, and trap him when it refroze.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|No! Not water!|Stagorr, just before his death|<ref name="E6" />}}
[[File:Stagorr1.png|thumb|left|upright|150px|Ice Demons had long, curved horns and sharp, icy teeth.]]
Ice Demons were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with an [[Water/Legends|ice]]-like appearance. Their bodies were [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] with musculature similar to other such species.<ref name="E6" /> They stood about two [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall,<ref name="height" /> and each of their hands had five digits, while each [[Limb/Legends|foot]] came to a single, sharp point. Their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were black with white centers, and their [[Nose/Legends|noses]] were pointed and hooked. Each Ice Demon had a large mouth filled with sharp, icicle-like [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], more on the bottom than on the top, and a white tongue. Their heads were crowned by two curved horns that pointed upward.<ref name="E6" />

Their ice-like traits set them apart from other humanoids. Ice Demons were white and icy, glistening blue in certain lighting conditions. The effect was so pronounced that icicles seemed to grow from their bodies. Their frosty natures made them resistant to severe cold.<ref name="E6" />

==History==
{{Quote|Stagorr dwells inside Ice Mountain! Stagorr, the evil Ice Demon! I myself imprisoned him there many years ago! And now, thanks to my carelessness, he may have found a way out!|Logray|<ref name="E6" />}}
[[File:Stagorr2.png|thumb|right|270px|Stagorr the Ice Demon was defeated and banished to Ice Mountain by the Ewok shaman Logray.]]
Years before [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date note">{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series, which includes [[Ewoks (1985) 6|''Ewoks'' 6]], to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of {{Film|IV}}, which equates to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]<ref name="Castaways of Endor">"[[Castaways of Endor]]" reveals that "[[Magic/Legends|magic]]" on Endor is actually [[totem magic]], a means of manipulating [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].</ref> Ice Demon [[Stagorr]] had established himself as a powerful [[Magic/Legends|sorcerer]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. That brought him into contact with [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. The two fought a [[Skirmish on Endor|magical duel]], during which Logray banished the Ice Demon to imprisonment within Endor's [[Ice Mountain]]. Stagorr never gave up hope of leaving his [[Prison/Legends|prison]], and he continued to spy on his old rival via a magical [[ice mirror]]. When he saw the young Ewok [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]] take [[Staff of Logray|Logray's staff]], Stagorr seized the moment to bring the Ewok to him via a [[Force storm (wormhole)|wind tunnel spell]], hoping to lure Logray back to Ice Mountain.<ref name="E6" />

The Ice Demon could not touch Logray's staff, the instrument that had banished him, so he put a hex on Warrick that forced the Ewok to obey his commands. As expected, Logray appeared at Ice Mountain to rescue his younger tribemate. Stagorr cloistered himself in his lair and sent his proxy, Warrick, to duel his old enemy. Nevertheless, Logray managed to counter Stagorr's every spell. Frustrated, the sorcerer finally showed himself, threatening to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Warrick if Logray did not relinquish the staff. Logray did so. The Ice Demon scoffed as Warrick attacked with a simple, fire-based magic trick, but the fire melted some of the Ice Mountain interior and caused liquid water to fall on Stagorr's body. As it refroze, he lost the ability to move.<ref name="E6" />

==Behind the scenes==
Stagorr the Ice Demon appears in [[Ewoks (1985) 6|''Ewoks'' 6]], an [[Star Wars comic books|issue]] of the [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]] [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] comic book written by [[David Manak]] and illustrated by [[Warren Kremer]].<ref name="E6" /> The comic was published on [[March 1]], [[1986]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54713/star_wars_ewoks_1985_6|text=Star Wars: Ewoks (1985) #6}}</ref> At one point in the story, Stagorr summons two icy humanoids to aid him, beings that Wicket W. Warrick dubs "[[ice creature]]s." The two beings are similar in appearance to Stagorr, although they lack horns, so they may represent other Ice Demons.<ref name="E6" />

==Appearances==
